title: cyathea tuerckheimii
intro: Cyathea tuerckheimii, also known as the Guatemalan tree fern, is a species of tree fern that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central America, specifically in Guatemala and other parts of the Mesoamerican region. The medicinal properties of Cyathea tuerckheimii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant is used in herbal preparations for various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, and can be found in teas, infusions, and extracts.
